message:
Perf: chunk object storage, batch reads, streaming clone, async worktree

R2 object chunking: bundle objects into ~2MB R2 values indexed via SQLite
object_chunks table. Reduces 10K R2 ops to ~50 on push. Batch readObjects
groups SHAs by chunk_key and fetches each chunk once.

Streaming clone response: use ReadableStream for sideband-wrapped packfile
instead of triple-buffering (packData + sidebandPkts + responseBody). Cuts
peak clone memory from ~3x packfile size to ~1x.

Packfile builder: accumulate compressed entries as chunk array, concatenate
once at end. Process objects in 500-SHA read batches so GC can reclaim
decompressed data between batches.

Async worktree materialization: defer via ctx.waitUntil() for pushes with
>500 objects. Checkout yields between read+write batches with setTimeout(0)
so DO can serve concurrent requests (clone) during background work.

SQLite-first hasObject: check chunk index before R2 HEAD, eliminates R2
round trips during fetch negotiation.

Results (5K files, 39MB incompressible data):
  Push:  3.4s  Clone: 3.1s  Stats: 447ms  Files: 435ms
